summary: Linked `src/melee/ft/ftwaitanim.c` by moving it from NonMatching to Matching in `configure.py`, with the main matching work completing `ftCo_8008A7A8`. The PR also cleaned up fighter wait-animation typing by replacing temporary `S_TEMP4`/`ftSubactionList` names with `Fighter_WaitAnimData` and `union CmdUnion*`, adjusted related uses in `fighter.c` and `ftdata.c`, and refreshed GALE01 symbol labels/scopes. The decomp.dev bot reported one new match in `main/melee/ft/ftwaitanim`, taking `ftCo_8008A7A8` from 99.86% to 100.00%, with +592 matched code bytes, +864 linked code bytes, and +72 linked data bytes.

key_files.why_it_matters: Changed `Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/ftwaitanim.c")` to `Object(Matching, "melee/ft/ftwaitanim.c")`, officially linking the unit.
Contained the function-level matching changes for `ftCo_8008A7A8`, including a manual inline of `ftCo_8008A6D8` and an assert macro adjustment.

Renamed the temporary wait-animation struct to `Fighter_WaitAnimData`, changed its script pointer field to `union CmdUnion*`, updated `ftData` and `Fighter` fields, and removed the now-unused `ftSubactionList` struct.
Updated wait-animation data casts and locals from `struct S_TEMP4*` to `struct Fighter_WaitAnimData*`.
Updated `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` local typing to use `struct Fighter_WaitAnimData*`.
Removed the obsolete forward typedef for `ftSubactionList`.
Updated symbol labels/scopes for data affected by linking, including replacing several `efAlt_*` local names with compiler-style `@NNN` labels and adding local data/bss labels.

what_changed: `ftwaitanim.c` was marked Matching in the build configuration.
`ftCo_8008A7A8` was completed to 100% matching according to the decomp.dev report.

`ftCo_8008A7A8` was restructured so the branch that chooses a new wait animation manually inlines the behavior of `ftCo_8008A6D8`; the diff includes a TODO noting this was done because it was the only way the author could make it match.
`OSReport("wait anim data illegal!!\n", max); __assert("ftwaitanim.c", 86, "0");` was replaced with `HSD_ASSERTREPORT(86, 0, "wait anim data illegal!!\n", max);`.
Temporary type name `struct S_TEMP4` was replaced throughout touched fighter code with `struct Fighter_WaitAnimData`.
`Fighter_WaitAnimData::xC` was typed as `union CmdUnion*`, allowing assignments such as `fp->x3E4_fighterCmdScript.u = anim->xC` without a cast.
The unused `ftSubactionList` forward declaration and struct definition were removed.
GALE01 symbols were regenerated/adjusted for linked data, including local-scope annotations and compiler-style local labels.
smart_moves: Gave an anonymous temporary struct a domain-specific name, `Fighter_WaitAnimData`, making later fighter wait-animation work easier to search and reason about.
Typed the command-script pointer field as `union CmdUnion*` instead of preserving a placeholder `ftSubactionList*`, reducing casts and aligning the field with `fighterCmdScript.u` usage.
Used `HSD_ASSERTREPORT` to combine the report/assert pattern in a way that matches the original codegen for the illegal wait-animation-data path.
Accepted a local manual inline in `ftCo_8008A7A8` and documented it with a TODO rather than hiding the matching trick; this makes the match reproducible for future cleanup.
Updated all adjacent users of the wait-animation data type (`ftwaitanim.c`, `ftdata.c`, `fighter.c`, `types.h`) instead of only changing the matching function.
decomp_lessons: For stubborn near-matches, inlining a small helper at one callsite can be necessary even when it is less clean C; document the reason with a TODO so future decompilers know it is intentional.
Replacing `OSReport` plus `__assert` with the project macro `HSD_ASSERTREPORT` can be required for matching assertion/reporting codegen.
Once a data struct’s role is understood, propagate the real name through headers and users; this improves both match quality and future reverse-engineering clarity.
If a pointer field is ultimately assigned into a known union-backed script field, type it as the target union pointer (`union CmdUnion*`) instead of inventing a single-byte placeholder struct.
Linking a previously nonmatching object may require symbol table cleanup for local data labels and scopes, not just C source edits.

naming_conventions.lesson: Prefer descriptive fighter-domain struct names once the role is known.
Use the project’s existing command-script union type for fighter script pointers.
When linking exposes compiler-local data, prefer generated local labels and correct `scope:local` annotations over misleading global-looking subsystem names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Marked `melee/ft/ftwaitanim.c` as Matching only after `ftCo_8008A7A8` reached 100%.
Manually inlined `ftCo_8008A6D8` inside `ftCo_8008A7A8` for the selected-animation path to satisfy codegen.

Introduced scoped temporaries (`temp`, `anim_id`) around the `getAnimID` loop to match register/lifetime behavior; evidence is the diff structure, but exact register intent is not available in the PR text.
Used `HSD_ASSERTREPORT(86, 0, ...)` to match the assert/report sequence at line 86.
Updated symbol labels in `.data`, `.sdata`, `.sdata2`, and `.bss` to reflect the linked output.

review_feedback.feedback: Automated report showed matched code 60.82% (+0.02%, +592 bytes), linked code 33.47% (+0.02%, +864 bytes), linked data 32.23% (+0.01%, +72 bytes), and one new match: `main/melee/ft/ftwaitanim` / `ftCo_8008A7A8` from 99.86% to 100.00%.
No human review comments or PR body rationale were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that clean up the TODO manual inline in `ftCo_8008A7A8`.
Search for remaining uses of `S_TEMP4` to ensure `Fighter_WaitAnimData` was fully propagated.
Search for remaining placeholder script structs like `ftSubactionList` that should use `union CmdUnion*`.
Compare other assertion/reporting near-matches that may need `HSD_ASSERTREPORT` instead of explicit `OSReport` plus `__assert`.
Audit other nonmatching fighter common files for helper-call versus manual-inline codegen issues.
